title: CapSched project gap classification schema
purpose: Classify preserved project overlay gap rows into semantic gap groups without treating gaps as removed obligations, implementation approval, runtime coverage, monitor verification, or protection evidence.

row_shape.gap_row_id: stable row id in generated gap classification output
row_shape.linux_id: Linux anchor id from the overlay row
row_shape.artifact_path: source artifact that produced the gap
row_shape.gap_class: future_linux_anchor | future_test_anchor | trace_plan_row | external_monitor_anchor | unsupported_extraction | unknown_gap
row_shape.required_resolution: what must exist before the gap can influence implementation
blocked_claims: claims that remain forbidden while this gap exists
row_shape.external_monitor_dependency: true if resolving this row depends on monitor/root-owned semantics
row_shape.duplicate_group_key: stable key used to group duplicate rows

group_shape.row_count: number of overlay rows in the group

hard_rules: A gap row remains an obligation or future anchor until a separate artifact resolves it.
Duplicate gap rows must be grouped by semantic gap id rather than counted as independent design problems.
A future Linux anchor is not authority; monitor-owned admission, epochs, replay, receipts, and revoke ordering remain external obligations.
A trace-plan row is not runtime coverage and does not approve tracefs execution or public tracepoint ABI.
A test/fault-injection gap must not change live monitor or Linux decisions.
